rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND TOWNSEND et al VS. ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication As To Plaintiffs Request For Punitive Damages
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, December 3, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Gardner Denver, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because it demonstrated that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that any of the gaskets at issue contained asbestos. Plaintiffs failed to submit admissible evidence to create a triable issue whether Mr. Townsend was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, while Mr. Bishop's deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. Townsend was exposed to defendant-attributable gaskets during the removal and/or installation of gaskets on Joy air compressors, plaintiffs did not submit admissible evidence to create a triable issue whether such gaskets contained asbestos. Plaintiffs' objections to the declarations of Sheldon Rabinovitz, Ph.D. and Howard Sandler, M.D. are sustained. In fact, the Court did not even consider these declarations. Mr. Finnamore's deposition testimony fails to create a triable issue because his testimony only covered Joy reciprocating air compressors but Mr. Bishop testified none of the air compressors at Hershey were reciprocating. Defendant's objections to Frank Bishop's declaration are sustained as to objections nos. 1-4 and overruled as to objections nos. 5-9. Defendant's objections to the declarations of John Fening and Stephen Compton, Ph.D. are sustained. These declarations lack foundation to establish that the compressors were reciprocating and to establish the air pressure and heat generated by the compressors, thus they lack foundation to opine on asbestos content of the gaskets. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
